name: Polar.sh API
what_it_does: Open-source monetization platform for developers and open source maintainers. Polar acts as Merchant of Record for global tax compliance, offers GitHub Sponsors integration, and provides subscription/one-time payment infrastructure via a clean API. Designed specifically for developers monetizing open-source projects, APIs, or dev tools without Stripe Tax complexity.
use_cases: Monetize AI agent tools and libraries with subscription or one-time payment billing via Polar's developer-friendly API
Gate agent service features behind paid tiers using Polar's subscription status API for license validation
Offer GitHub Sponsors-style funding for open-source agent infrastructure with Polar's GitHub integration
Process global payments for agent-powered SaaS with automatic tax handling via Polar's Merchant of Record model
Create benefit-gated access (Discord roles, file downloads, private repos) for paid agent tool subscribers
not_for: High-volume enterprise billing with complex pricing — Stripe Billing or Chargebee have more flexibility
Non-developer products — Polar is specifically positioned for developer tools and open source projects
Teams needing extensive reporting and revenue analytics — more established platforms have richer analytics
best_when: You're an open-source developer or small dev tools team wanting frictionless, tax-compliant billing with GitHub integration and a developer-first experience.
avoid_when: You need complex pricing logic, large-scale enterprise billing, or consumer-facing payments — Stripe or Lemon Squeezy are more established.

auth.notes: API key for server-side access. OAuth 2.0 for acting on behalf of users. OpenAPI spec available. Scopes for granular permission control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free to start: 5% fee on transactions + payment processing
pricing.paid_tiers: No monthly fee — revenue share model (5% fee); lower fees with volume
pricing.notes: Open source platform itself is free. Polar takes 5% of transactions as MoR. No monthly subscription required. Lower overhead than Lemon Squeezy Plus plan for very small volumes.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec available — REST API is well-documented. Webhook payloads are typed and documented. SDK provides TypeScript types for type-safe integration.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Checkout creation is not idempotent. Order and subscription queries are idempotent. Agents should avoid recreating checkouts for the same intent.
known_agent_gotchas: Polar is newer and less battle-tested than Stripe or Lemon Squeezy — evaluate maturity before mission-critical billing
GitHub integration is a core feature but requires GitHub account — not suitable for non-GitHub projects
Benefit delivery (Discord roles, file downloads) requires Polar to manage the benefit — some customization is limited
International payment support depends on Polar's payment processor coverage — verify supported countries
Webhook events should be validated using the provided signing secret before processing

security_score_components.security_notes: Open source for full auditability. PCI-DSS compliant (payment processing delegated). GDPR compliant. OAuth 2.0 with scopes. Webhook signing prevents event forgery.
